fix_suggestion.reason: The root cause of the compilation errors is the use of incorrect ASN.1 generated type names for ProtocolExtensionContainer structs in the F1AP UE context code. The compiler explicitly suggests replacing all occurrences of types named 'F1AP_ProtocolExtensionContainer_10696P60_t', 'F1AP_ProtocolExtensionContainer_10696P82_t', and 'F1AP_ProtocolExtensionContainer_10696P83_t' with the correct types 'F1AP_ProtocolExtensionContainer_19016P60_t', 'F1AP_ProtocolExtensionContainer_19016P82_t', and 'F1AP_ProtocolExtensionContainer_19016P83_t' respectively. These type mismatches cause unknown type name errors and invalid member accesses (e.g., 'list' member not found). The errors occur in multiple functions that encode and decode F1AP messages related to CU-to-DU RRC information and DRB setup. Correcting these type names to the compiler-suggested ones will resolve the build failures.

fix_suggestion.root_cause_analysis: The compilation errors stem from incorrect ASN.1 generated type names used for ProtocolExtensionContainer structs in the F1AP UE context code. The types with suffixes '10696P60', '10696P82', and '10696P83' do not exist or are outdated. The compiler suggests replacing them with the correct types having suffixes '19016P60', '19016P82', and '19016P83' respectively. These types are generated by ASN.1 compiler from the 3GPP ASN.1 definitions and must match exactly. Using wrong type names causes unknown type errors and invalid member accesses (like accessing 'list' member on a non-structure). Correcting these type names to the compiler-suggested ones resolves the build failures and ensures proper structure layout and member access.

Check the ASN.1 generated header files for the correct type names for these containers.

Apply the compiler-suggested replacements for all occurrences of the incorrect types.
Rebuild the project to verify that the unknown type errors and member access errors are resolved.
Run unit tests or integration tests to confirm functional correctness after type fixes.
